seoTitle: How to Create a Gantt Chart in Trello
metaDescription: Build a Gantt chart from your Trello cards using Power-Ups like Planyway or Ganttify to map dependencies and project timelines.
intro: Trello doesn't include a native Gantt chart, but pairing it with a dedicated Power-Up lets you visualize project milestones and dependencies without leaving your board. This guide covers enabling a Gantt Power-Up and mapping your cards into a timeline.

steps.heading: Open the Power-Up directory
steps.body: On your board, click Power-Ups to access the unified directory of available integrations.

steps.heading: Add a Gantt chart tool
steps.body: Search for a certified option such as Planyway, Ganttify, or BigPicture, and add it to your board.

steps.heading: Sync your card data
steps.body: Let the tool map your existing card start dates and due dates onto a chronological grid.

steps.heading: Build task dependencies
steps.body: Link parent cards to child tasks to establish dependencies and prevent scheduling bottlenecks.

steps.heading: Customize your chart view
steps.body: Filter your Gantt view by team member, active list, or priority label to focus on what matters most.

steps.heading: Adjust for shifting deadlines
steps.body: Use the tool's rescheduling features to keep your timeline accurate as milestone dates change.

faq.question: Does Trello have a built-in Gantt chart feature?
faq.answer: No, you'll need a Power-Up like Planyway, Ganttify, or BigPicture to render an interactive Gantt chart from your cards.

faq.question: How does the Gantt chart get its data?
faq.answer: It pulls start dates and due dates directly from your existing Trello cards to build the timeline automatically.

faq.question: Can I set dependencies between tasks?
faq.answer: Yes, most Gantt Power-Ups let you link parent cards to child tasks to model dependencies.

faq.question: Can I filter the Gantt chart by team member?
faq.answer: Yes, you can typically filter or group the chart by assignee, list, or priority label.
